Art title 1: Station 4 Jesus is Denied by Peter
Artist Statement 1: The eyes of the horrified Peter are also the eyes of the rooster thatconvicts Peter of his denial. And Jesus looks at him, sadly from thecenter. We can see Peter denying Jesus to the three people whoasked Peter if he knew him. We can see his silhouette by the firewhere Jesus looked at him, the moment the rooster crowed.
Story Title again: ANIMATION: Station 4 Jesus is Denied by Peter
excerpt:
(See video below) Now Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. One of the maids came over to him and said, "You too were with Jesus the Galilean." But he denied it in front of everyone, saying, "I do not know what you are talking about!" As he went out to the gate, another girl saw him and said to those who were there, "This man was with Jesus the Nazorean." Again he denied it with an oath, "I do not know the man!" A little later the bystanders came over and said to Peter, "Surely you too are one of them; even your speech gives you away." At that he began to curse and to swear, "I do not know the man." And immediately a cock crowed. Then Peter remembered the word that Jesus had spoken: "Before the cock crows you will deny me three times." He went out and began to weep bitterly. 
(Matthew 26: 69--75, NAB)


Lord, grant us the gift of honesty, that we may
not fear to speak the truth even when difficult.


First we see the three people asking Peter if he knows Jesus, and the three times Peter denies
him. Who is someone you are embarrassed to be seen around? Is it justified? What if they needed
help at the expense of your reputation? If God made it clear that you should, would you be
able to step in? Imagine what this might look like, and with whom.

Look at Peter now, warming his hands by the fire, being torn by his desires to stay unnoticed,and to help one of his closest friends. Imagine when a servant girl says, loud enough for the three or four others at the fire to hear, "Weren't you friends with that Jesus?" If he said yes, he'd suddenly be the center of attention and judgment. Can you think of situations in your life where you were put in a position like this?

Now think of that moment, after a couple others had asked and he denied knowing Jesus three times, when the rooster crowed. At that moment he remembered the supper the previous night when he had told Jesus,"I would never deny you!" But Jesus had replied, "Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times, Peter."
Think of the horror that poured over him when he realized he had done exactly what he had sworn he would never do. When has this been you? Or when have you minimized your friendship with someone because others were criticizing them? Recently at church, with family, at work, or long ago in the school yard? When have you betrayed a friend for a romantic interest? Or to position yourself better on social media? Or to shuck blame at your job or home.


Imagine that moment when Jesus looked at Peter and they shared full knowing of what he had done. When have you felt betrayed? Did you feel something like anger or loneliness for being left out to dry, when you had done so much for them?
 Jesus felt this too. 

Now listen to Jesus's response.


The next time they talked was also by a fire, this time cooking fish on the beach for breakfast after Jesus had died and risen. Jesus asked, "Peter, do you love me?" 

"Yes, Lord." 

"Then feed my sheep, Peter." A second time Jesus asked him, "Peter, do you love me more than these?" 

Peter replied, "You know that I do." 

"Then feed my sheep, Peter." Then a third and final time, "Peter do you love me more than these people around us?" 

Peter was hurt. "Lord, you know all things. You know I love you." 

"Then feed my sheep, Peter." 

Jesus redeemed Peter's failure by letting him try again. And through it, he called him to be an integral part of a much bigger story. 


There is forgiveness. He calls us to forgive. He calls us to love. He is calling us to Easter.

Amen.
3dNotes2: Mt. Carmel: view from Mt. Carmel
3dNotes3: Shunem: Where Elijah prophesied the Shunemite woman would have a child. This is also the same area where he raised that child from the dead (as referenced in the 2 Kings scripture today)
